conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions. Let me start with:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex - he's 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I have a few questions to help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, full-time non-advanced education such as school, college A-levels, Scottish Highers, or approved training courses)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship and living situation with Alex.

**Do you live with Alex, and are you responsible for him?** (This means you're responsible for things like providing for his care, food, clothing, and general welfare)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me and I'm responsible for him - I take care of all his needs, food, clothing, and everything else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to check your residency status.

**Are you currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to reside here?** (For example, are you a UK citizen, have settled status, or have another immigration status that allows you to live in the UK?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester, and I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. I have just a couple more questions to ensure I have all the information needed.

**Is Alex living with anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for him?** (For example, does he split time between two households, or is anyone else also claiming to be responsible for him?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not. Alex lives with me full-time and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Is Alex currently in hospital or in local authority care for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not. He's just living at home with me and going to college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 17 years old and studying A-levels at sixth form college, which counts as approved full-time non-advanced education
✓ You live with Alex and are responsible for his care and welfare
✓ You live in the UK (Manchester) and have the right to reside here
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
✓ Alex is not in hospital or local authority care

Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training, so Alex qualifies. You meet all the eligibility requirements to claim Child Benefit for him.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the government website or by post. If you have any other questions, feel free to ask!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. He is 17 years old and in approved full-time non-advanced education (studying A-levels at sixth form college), which means he qualifies as an eligible child under the age extension rules (Child Benefit can continue for 16-19 year olds in approved education or training). The claimant lives with Alex in the UK, is responsible for him (providing care, food, clothing, and welfare), has the right to reside in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital or local authority care. All conditions under the Child Benefit eligibility rules are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex. Alex is in approved education (A-levels), lives with the claimant who is responsible for him, the claimant has the right to reside in the UK, and no one else is claiming for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
